PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-14-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Baer for Northern California Broadcasting Inc., St. Anthony Foundation and Mechanics Institute. Eugene Hahm for PETA and SFSPCA. Betty Epstein for 9 of the Charitable Beneficiaries.

Counsel informed the Court that the issues regarding Ms. Epstein's Clients were resolved, that they were working on a global settlement, that there were disputes between Mr Hahm's Clients and Mr. Baer's Clients and that some discovery still needed to be conducted. The matter was referred to the department of the Presiding Judge (206) to be set for trial for the issue regarding the will contest, (order for the same signed to be signed).

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Dec-14-2009 Text Ruling

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (279 Topaz Way San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar Oct-28-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for Debra Dolch.

The sale was confirmed at $850,000.00. An additional bond in the amount of $440,000.00 is required. The Court granted the petition, (subject to a written order).

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (279 Topaz Way San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar Oct-14-2009 Continued To Oct-28-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for Debra Dolch.

Ms. Rasch requested a continuance stating that the Inventory and Appraisal had not come back from the Referee. The matter was continued to October 28, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Jung.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Sep-14-2009 Continued To Dec-14-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-14-2009 Continued To Dec-14-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for Debra Dolch (Special Administrator). David Baer for Northern California Public Broadcasting, Mechanics Institute and St. Anthony Foundation. Eugene Hahm for SPCA and PETA. Stacey Nelson for Nine Beneficiaries of the 1987 will.

Mr. Baer stated that the matter was ready to be set for a hearing and that discovery was minimal. Ms. Nelson stated that she was open to setting the matter far out, that discovery needed to be conducted and that she was prepared to send a settlement proposal to Mr. Baer. The Court stated that the underlying petitions were not sent to the Heirs and that they needed to be served. Ms. Nelson stated that the petition was filed by the Public Administrator, that they needed to serve but that she would be happy to do so if appropriate. The matter was continued to December 14, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court approved Ms. Rasch's request for general powers, extended the letters of Special Administration and signed the order for the same in open court which was reviewed by all Parties.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith./J. Shannon.
